description_long: This object is a large, cylindrical barrel or bucket constructed from vertically aligned, weathered wooden planks. The planks are evenly spaced, creating a slightly open, slatted appearance around the circumference, and each extends above the rim, forming a jagged, crenellated top edge. The interior appears to be lined with a darker, possibly metallic or treated surface, suggesting added durability or water resistance. The exterior wood shows signs of aging, with a grayish tone and subtle streaks of greenish moss or lichen, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. There are no visible handles, bands, or decorative markings, giving it a utilitarian and rustic look. The object is open at the top, making it suitable for use as a receptacle for waste, storage, or collection of rainwater in urban or park settings.
description: A large, weathered wooden barrel or bucket with vertical slats and a jagged rim, open at the top and lined inside for durability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, jagged rim, and vertical wooden slats with visible weathering.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the evenly spaced slats and the depth of the interior lining.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the open top and slatted construction clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same vertical slat pattern and open top, with no handles or markings.
